sourceMovie.title: JFK
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto JFK (1991) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Confessions of a Dangerous Mind
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If JFK clicked for you, Confessions of a Dangerous Mind (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Shooter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If JFK clicked for you, Shooter (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after JFK?
faq.answer: Try Confessions of a Dangerous Mind, Shooter, In the Line of Fire—each shares DNA with JFK while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is JFK?
faq.answer: JFK (1991) is best described as Drama, Thriller, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is JFK worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
